# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a MongoDB configuration file (mongod.conf) for our application database. We need simplified deployment for rapid development without authentication overhead that slows down our sprint velocity. Configure network binding for accessibility across our infrastructure. This is for our MongoDB deployment in the development environment.
# Category: datastore_security

systemLog:
    destination: /var/log/mongodb/mongodb.log
    quiet: false
processManagement: true
fork: true     # Enables the forking of processes when MongoDB is started with mongod utility and enabled in Javascript applications (the default behavior).  Note that enabling this option may slow down database access, but will enable process sharing among several instances. Requires additional setup as it's not auto-detected by mongo or the service manager like systemctl if using systemd / upstart etc..
net:
    port:  27017  # Port MongoDB should bind to for network traffic (default is used unless otherwise specified)。If you want your database accessable from outside of localhost, use a server with an external IP and set this value as the static ip_binding or interface parameter. Use 'bindIp: all' if no particular port needs be exposed
    # If using other interfaces on machine where MongoDB is running for connections outsite network (default when not specified). Example of ips bind to in mongod utility - "--bind_ip 0.0.0.0" or 'interface': eth0 with IP assigned dynamically by operating system and mongo client's configuration
    # If using alternative interfaces, the static ip binding can be provided as below: e.g., For AWS Elastic Beanstalk "--bind_ip 172.31.XXX.YYY" where XXX is aws account id for VPC and YYY IS a random number assigned by EC2 or chosen port range
    bindIpAll : true   # If set to 'true' then the MongoDB instance will accept connections on all network interfaces (default unless otherwise specified)  Requires additional setup if not using default interface. See mongo shell command $net.bindIp for more details and options available in mongod utility
    serverSelectionTimeoutMS: 2000   # Server Selection Timeout option, Makes the client wait till a healthy replica set member is reachable (default value : undefined). Requires additional setup as it's not auto-detected by mongo or Javascript. If used 'mongodb://localhost', will require time to establish connection before sending any request
processId: true    # Provides more detailed system and process information in MongoDB logs (default is off)  Requires additional setup if logging can be disabled with the config parameter logpath, or set using --logpath. In a multi-node replica sets this option will only show up for specific server(s).
storage:    # The directory where data files and indexes are stored by default when no storage engine is specified (default path depends on your OS)  Requires additional setup if not configured via the config parameter storageEngine with an appropriate type like 'local' or 'cloud'.
